title: nvm_list cursor-stuck guard prevents infinite loop on misbehaving server
description: The guard at nvm.rs line 178 checks out_id == start_id && start_id != 0 and returns ProtocolError, preventing an infinite loop if the server returns the same cursor ID without advancing. The start_id != 0 carve-out correctly avoids false-tripping on the initial call (where start_id = 0 = WH_NVM_ID_INVALID). A contributor might suggest always checking out_id == start_id, but that would misfire on the empty-NVM first-call case.
